query: Why is HF better for local RAG deployment?
query_rewrite: "Advantages of using HF for local RAG deployment" "HF benefits in local RAG setup"
query_rewrite_raw: "Advantages of using HF for local RAG deployment" "HF benefits in local RAG setup"
retrieval_results: For deployment on AArch64 platforms, such as RK3568 boards, containerization becomes even more important because it reduces manual configuration drift across devices.
A local HuggingFace embedding model may be suitable for offline experimentation, while Cohere may be more practical for remote or hybrid deployments.
However, containerization does not remove hardware constraints. On a lightweight board with limited memory and storage, not every model that works on a desktop environment will remain feasible.
